// 01 — About Me

Who I Am

I'm Gowtham S, a Electronics & Communication Engineering graduate from Loyola Institute of Technology, Chennai — completed my degree in May 2026 with a CGPA of 8.1.

My engineering journey spans across embedded systems, IoT automation, and VLSI hardware design. I've built a waste segregation system with Arduino, designed an FPGA-based hardware security system, and gained real-world exposure at Schneider Electric, Wipro Infrastructure, and Sungwoo Hitech.

I'm actively seeking Graduate Engineer Trainee (GET) roles in core electronics, embedded systems, and manufacturing sectors across India. I am open to relocation anywhere in the country.

Languages: Tamil (Native)  |  English (Professional)

// 03 — Industry Experience

Internships

▹ July 2025 — August 2025
Quality Checker Intern
⬡ Sungwoo Hitech — Automotive Component Manufacturing
  • Performed quality inspection of electronic and mechanical automotive components against production tolerances and specifications.
  • Identified, documented, and escalated defects — contributing to measurable improvements in defect pass-through rate on the assembly line.
  • Gained hands-on exposure to quality control frameworks in a high-volume automotive manufacturing environment.
  • Developed understanding of ISO quality standards and systematic defect classification methods.

⬡ Wipro Infrastructure — Enterprise IT & Technology (60-Day Program)

  • Supported real-time system health monitoring and participated in documenting resolution procedures for recurring incidents.
  • Gained practical exposure to enterprise networking, structured cabling, and data center operational protocols.
  • Collaborated in a cross-functional team environment managing multiple concurrent infrastructure tasks.

// 04 — Hardware & Software Projects

Projects

01
🗑️
Smart Waste Segregation Bin using Arduino

An intelligent automated waste classification system that eliminates the need for manual sorting by detecting material type and directing waste into the correct bin in real time.

ArduinoC Programming IR SensorUltrasonic Sensor Servo MotorIoT
  • Classifies Dry, Wet, and Metal waste using multi-sensor fusion
  • Servo motors auto-route waste to correct bin without human input
  • Full embedded C logic — sensor polling, decision tree, actuator control
  • Applicable to smart city waste management infrastructure
02
🔐
VLSI-Based Hardware Security System (FPGA)

A hardware-level security architecture implemented on FPGA to detect and mitigate power side-channel attacks in digital integrated circuits — a critical challenge in modern chip security.

FPGA (Xilinx)Verilog VLSI DesignClock Modulation Pattern MatchingPower Analysis
  • Detects power side-channel attacks via dynamic power consumption patterns
  • Configurable clock modulation to randomize timing and reduce leakage
  • Real-time pattern matching engine identifies abnormal power signatures
  • Demonstrates VLSI design flow from RTL to hardware implementation
